title: Where are we going with genomics in plant pathogenic bacteria?
abstract: Genome sequencing is commonly used in research laboratories right now thanks to the rise of high-throughput sequencing with higher speed and output-to-cost ratios. Here, we summarized the application of genomics in different aspects of plant bacterial pathosystems. Genomics has been used in studying the mechanisms of plant-bacteria interactions, and host specificity. It also helps with taxonomy, study of non-cultured bacteria, identification of causal agent, single cell sequencing, population genetics, and meta-transcriptomic. Overall, genomics has significantly improved our understanding of plant-microbe interaction.
